chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Criminal Bail Application No.390 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATUREs OF JUDGEs 1. For orders on M.A. No.2305/2025 (U/A) 2. For orders on M.A. No.2306/2025 (Exp/A) 3. For hearing of bail application ------------------------- 12.02.2025 Mr. Sajid Ali, advocate for applicant ------------------------------------ Applicant/accused Syed Hamza Farooq son of Syed Farooq Shah seeks protective bail in FIR No.112/2025, registered at P.S. City Mansehra for offence under sections 295-A, 298-A, PPC and Section 11 of PECA Act. Learned counsel for the applicant submits that the applicant/accused is innocent and has been falsely implicated in this case due to mala fide intentions and ulterior motive; that he is a law abiding citizen and intends to surrender before the trial Court concerned but he apprehends his arrest at the hands of local police as such he seeks protective bail to enable him to appear before the concerned trial Court. Without touching the merits of the cas e, protective bail is granted to applicant/accused Syed Hamza Farooq son of Syed Farooq Shah, for a period of six days from today, subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.25,000/- (Rupees twenty five thousand) and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Nazir of this Court. Applicant/accused is directed to surrender before the trial Court concerned in the aforesaid period and join the trial forthwith. This order shall cease to have its effect after expiry of six days from today or whenever applicant/accused surrenders before the trial Court, which one occurs earlier. It is made clear, in case, applicant/accused fails to surrender before the trial Court in the aforesaid period, his bail bond shall stands automatically forfeited.

This order shall cease to have its effect after expiry of six days from today or whenever applicant/accused surrenders before the trial Court, which one occurs earlier. It is made clear, in case, applicant/accused fails to surrender before the trial Court in the aforesaid period, his bail bond shall stands automatically forfeited. The instant criminal bail application is accordingly disposed of. J U D G E Gulsher/PS
